content.problemStatement:
Chef is the financial incharge of Chefland and one of his duties is identifying if any company has gained a monopolistic advantage in the market.

There are exactly 333 companies in the market each of whose revenues are denoted by R1R_1R1​, R2R_2R2​ and R3R_3R3​ respectively. A company is said to have a monopolistic advantage if its revenue is **strictly** greater than the sum of the revenues of its competitors.

Given the revenue of the 333 companies, help Chef determine if any of them has a monopolistic advantage.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input containing three space separated integers R1R_1R1​, R2R_2R2​ and R3R_3R3​ denoting the revenue of the three companies respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output YES\texttt{YES}YES if any of the companies has a monopolistic advantage over its competitors, else output NO\texttt{NO}NO.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings YeS\texttt{YeS}YeS, yEs\texttt{yEs}yEs, yes\texttt{yes}yes and YES\texttt{YES}YES will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers R1, R2 and R3 – the revenues of the three companies.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if any company has revenue strictly greater than the sum of the other two, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: No revenue exceeds the sum of the other two, so output NO.
Test case 2: 4 > 1+2, so YES.
Test case 3: 10 > 2+3, so YES.
Test case 4: No company dominates, so NO.

solution.approach: For each test case read the three revenues. A company has a monopolistic advantage if its revenue is strictly larger than the sum of the other two revenues. We simply check the three possible conditions: R1 > R2 + R3, R2 > R1 + R3, or R3 > R1 + R2. If any condition holds, print YES, otherwise print NO. The algorithm runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
